title: Expressive Hatching
description: Ce style émule un dessin traditionnel au graphite ou au fusain sur papier texturé. Il s'appuie sur des hachures parallèles et croisées vigoureuses et expressives pour créer du volume et des ombres, laissant le ton chaud du papier comme source de lumière principale pour créer une atmosphère brute, cinétique et intime.
description_en: This style emulates a traditional graphite or charcoal life drawing on textured paper. It relies on vigorous, expressive parallel hatching and cross-hatching to build volume and shadow, leaving the warm paper tone as the primary light source to create a raw, kinetic, and intimate atmosphere.
description_fr: Ce style émule un dessin traditionnel au graphite ou au fusain sur papier texturé. Il s'appuie sur des hachures parallèles et croisées vigoureuses et expressives pour créer du volume et des ombres, laissant le ton chaud du papier comme source de lumière principale pour créer une atmosphère brute, cinétique et intime.
prompt: Traditional dry medium sketch, executing a masterful graphite and charcoal drawing on rough textured paper. Apply this style to the complete scene described by {{subject}}, preserving its factual content, camera angle, framing, spatial layout, and stated exclusions. The image MUST occupy the absolute ENTIRE dedicated space as a canvas rule: NO borders, NO frames, NO white margins, NO canvas edges, FULL BLEED to all four corners. The rendering technique relies entirely on vigorous, expressive parallel hatching and cross-hatching rather than smooth blending. Shadows and volumes are built through the dense accumulation of raw, kinetic, unblended strokes, utilizing varying pencil pressure to create deep, rich darks and delicate mid-tones. The underlying paper provides a warm {{primary_color}} base that acts as the primary light source and highlights, while {{secondary_color}} and intense {{accent_color}} are used for the varying hardness of the graphite marks. The lighting anatomy features strong directional contrast, defined by the stark transition from negative space to densely hatched shadow areas. Strokes follow a dynamic, rhythmic 45-degree angle in negative spaces while contour hatching wraps organically around the forms of the scene. The materiality of the piece highlights the tactile quality of a dry medium dragging across visible paper tooth, complete with minor raw graphite dust artifacts. Edges are defined by bold, variable-weight contour lines and ragged hatching drop-offs, creating a spontaneous, intimate, and highly skilled academic study atmosphere., traditional sketch, graphite drawing, expressive hatching, charcoal lines, academic study, rough paper texture, dry medium, unblended strokes, full bleed, edge-to-edge, no border, no frame
prompt_removebg: Traditional dry medium sketch, executing a masterful graphite and charcoal drawing composited on a clean {{background_color}} surface. Apply this style to the complete scene described by {{subject}}, preserving its factual content, camera angle, framing, spatial layout, and stated exclusions. The rendering technique relies entirely on vigorous, expressive parallel hatching and cross-hatching rather than smooth blending. Shadows and volumes are built through the dense accumulation of raw, kinetic, unblended strokes, utilizing varying pencil pressure to create deep, rich darks and delicate mid-tones. The underlying paper provides a warm {{primary_color}} base that acts as the primary light source and highlights, while {{secondary_color}} and intense {{accent_color}} are used for the varying hardness of the graphite marks. The lighting anatomy features strong directional contrast, defined by the stark transition from negative space to densely hatched shadow areas. Strokes follow a dynamic, rhythmic 45-degree angle in negative spaces while contour hatching wraps organically around the forms of the scene. The materiality of the piece highlights the tactile quality of a dry medium dragging across visible paper tooth, complete with minor raw graphite dust artifacts. Edges are defined by bold, variable-weight contour lines and ragged hatching drop-offs that interact directly with the flat {{background_color}} backdrop. Intentional overflow is permitted where parts of the subject's sketched contours can extend beyond the visible frame, cropped naturally by the image boundaries., traditional sketch, graphite drawing, expressive hatching, charcoal lines, academic study, rough paper texture, dry medium, unblended strokes
background_prompt: An abstract, atmospheric environment rendered purely through traditional graphite and charcoal sketching techniques. Vigorous, rhythmic parallel hatching at a 45-degree angle builds deep shadows and textured gradients across the composition. The rough tooth of the paper is highly visible, providing a warm {{primary_color}} base illuminated by negative space. Dense clusters of {{secondary_color}} and {{accent_color}} strokes create a sense of spatial depth and stark directional lighting, forming an empty, expressive backdrop of raw, unblended dry medium marks without any focal subjects, borders, or framing elements.
background_prompt_removebg: An abstract, atmospheric environment rendered purely through traditional graphite and charcoal sketching techniques. Vigorous, rhythmic parallel hatching at a 45-degree angle builds deep shadows and textured gradients across the composition. The rough tooth of the paper is highly visible, providing a warm {{primary_color}} base illuminated by negative space. Dense clusters of {{secondary_color}} and {{accent_color}} strokes create a sense of spatial depth and stark directional lighting, forming an expressive backdrop of raw, unblended dry medium marks. In the foreground, a loose, expressive border of heavy graphite cross-hatching and spontaneous charcoal contour lines frames the invisible central subject. The rough, ragged edges of this drawn frame transition abruptly into a clean {{background_color}} surface, creating a stark contrast between the textured sketch environment and the flat, solid color backdrop.
